title: Supplier Intelligence Dossier v2
description: Structured data output for a single-supplier intelligence dossier. Neutral upstream intelligence asset — no strategy, displacement, or attack language. JSON is canonical; the human-readable report is rendered from this data by a deterministic template engine. Other agents consume this directly.

meta.description: Report metadata — when it was built, who it covers, what taxonomy version, when sections go stale

supplierName.description: Full legal name of the supplier

generatedBy.description: Who or what produced this dossier

refreshedFrom.description: If this is a refresh, the version it built on

taxonomyVersion.description: Version of service-taxonomy.json used for classification, e.g. 2.0

sourcesSummary.description: Quick count of raw material the AI had to work with

ftsRecords.description: Number of FTS award/notice records for this supplier

chPopulated.description: Whether Companies House data was available

annualReportYears.description: How many years of annual report data were available

pressReleases.description: Number of press releases / public statements reviewed

refreshDue.description: When each section goes stale — checked when the dossier is pulled for an active pursuit

versionLog.description: What changed in each version — most recent first

identity.description: Core facts about who this organisation is

ultimateParentGroup.description: If parentGroup is an intermediate holding company, the top of the chain

registeredAddress.description: Companies House registered office — may differ from trading HQ

listedExchange.description: If listed — e.g. LSE, AIM. Null if not listed

employeeCount.description: Total headcount, UK or group as available

scores.description: Six headline scores — three AI-assessed (qualitative judgement), three computed by the renderer from structured data. All display as a 4-colour RAG rating (red/amber/green/grey) with a one-sentence rationale. Factor-level detail stays in JSON for Agent 3 machine consumption but never surfaces as a number in the human report.

sectorStrength.description: AI-assessed. How deeply embedded in UK public sector — framework positions, Crown Rep status, contract longevity, buyer relationships

scrutinyExposure.description: AI-assessed. How much public scrutiny, regulatory attention, and media risk — NAO reports, PAC hearings, delivery failures

strategicIdentityConfidence.description: AI-assessed. How clearly we can characterise what this supplier is and where it's heading — coherence across narrative, contracts, and strategy

supplierBreadth.description: Computed. How diversified across service lines, sectors, and buyers — derived from serviceLineProfile, contracts, and frameworks

serviceLineConcentration.description: Computed. How dependent on a single service line — derived from serviceLineProfile contract values

evidenceQuality.description: Computed. How much of this dossier is backed by hard evidence — derived from evidenceRegister tier distribution, confidence, and claim types

serviceLineProfile.description: Decomposition against the platform service taxonomy. Only categories with evidence appear — no not_evidenced padding.

revenueIndicator.description: Qualitative — precise per-service-line revenue rarely available

flagshipEvidence.description: Single strongest proof point — a named contract, framework position, or public statement

contractCount.description: How many contracts in the FTS data map to this service line

notableContracts.description: References to contracts in the contracts array by title or OCID

financialTrajectory.description: Multi-year financial trend analysis. Minimum 3 years, target 5. Replaces the single-year snapshot from v1.

disclosureLevel.description: Sets expectations for how much data follows

currency.description: GBP unless non-UK parent reports in another currency

trajectoryNarrative.description: 2-3 paragraphs: what changed over the period, what's driving it, what the trend signals

years.description: Year objects, most recent first. All numeric fields nullable — Companies House-only suppliers may have limited data.

source.description: e.g. Annual Report 2025, Companies House filing

leverage.description: Ratio — net debt / EBITDA or similar

significantEvents.description: M&A, restructuring, write-downs, disposals that year

contracts.description: Structured contract data enriched with service-line tags, strategic importance, and business context. Neutral — no defensibility or attack language.

ocid.description: OCDS identifier — ties back to the competitive intel DB

sector.description: Public sector domain — defence, health, central gov, local gov, justice, etc

serviceLines.description: Array of categoryId refs from service-taxonomy.json — a contract can span multiple categories

routeToMarket.description: How they got it — framework call-off, open competition, single source, incumbent rebid

businessContext.description: 1-2 sentences: why does this contract matter? What does it prove about the supplier?

capabilityProof.description: What capability does winning/holding this contract demonstrate

deliveryNotes.description: Factual observations about delivery — NAO findings, service credits, public reporting. Neutral.

successorRouteExpectation.description: When this expires, what's the likely re-procurement route

frameworks.description: Framework memberships enriched with service-line tags, supplier position, and significance assessment

serviceLines.description: Array of categoryId refs from service-taxonomy.json

estimatedValue.description: Framework ceiling value if published, GBP

callOffActivity.description: Is this framework actually generating deal flow

significance.description: How much does this supplier rely on this framework

leadership.description: Key personnel — public sector experience as a descriptive string, not a boolean

startDate.description: When they took this role, if known

publicSectorBackground.description: One sentence describing public sector experience, or null if none known

partnerships.description: Alliance and subcontracting relationships with qualitative depth assessment replacing the v1 numeric strengthScore

serviceLines.description: Array of categoryId refs from service-taxonomy.json

evidence.description: What proves this partnership exists — a joint bid, a press release, a framework lot

marketVoiceSignals.description: How the supplier talks about themselves vs what the evidence shows. Narrative-to-reality alignment analysis from annual reports, CEO statements, press releases, thought leadership.

alignmentVerdict.description: Headline: does reality match the story

themes.description: One entry per narrative theme detected. Open vocabulary — not restricted to a fixed enum.

theme.description: What the supplier is claiming — e.g. AI-led transformation

supportingEvidence.description: AI characterisation of what supports the claim

contradictingEvidence.description: AI characterisation of what undermines it, or null

bidOutcomeSignals.description: Pattern analysis from FTS procurement data. Observable patterns, not win rates. Strict inference-vs-confirmation honesty.

description.description: Plain English — e.g. Serco lost 3 of 5 NHS FM rebids to Mitie between 2023-2025

inferenceLevel.description: Strict honesty: did we see a loss or infer one

dateRange.description: Period over which the pattern was observed

sectorConcentration.description: Award distribution across public sector domains

riskExposureProfile.description: Neutral factor-level risk and exposure assessment. Replaces v1 vulnerabilityMap. No scores, no weights, no rolled-up number — Agent 3 computes its own rollups from these factors.

summaryNarrative.description: 2-3 paragraphs: the overall risk and exposure picture

factors.description: Expected factors: margin_pressure, contract_friction, concentration_risk, delivery_complexity, commercial_stance, organisational_change, buyer_relationship_stress, partner_dependency. Open — AI may add others.

factorId.description: Identifier — one of the expected set or an AI-identified factor

narrative.description: 2-3 sentences: what was found, what it means

signalSynthesis.description: Curation layer — pulls forward-looking signals from other sections into a single watchlist with review dates. No independent research.

signals.description: Priority-ordered list of signals to monitor

claimType.description: Signals are by definition not verified facts

triggerCondition.description: What would confirm or invalidate this

evidenceRegister.description: Per-claim evidence ledger for material claims only — anything feeding a score, structured field, contract row, financial metric, or executive summary. Supporting narrative prose uses inline citation, not a ledger entry.

fieldPath.description: Which schema field this supports — e.g. financialTrajectory.years[0].revenue or scores.sectorStrength

effectiveDate.description: What date the claim is true as of — distinct from publication date

isDirect.description: Did the source state this directly, or was it inferred

staleAfterDays.description: How long before this claim should be rechecked

contradictionFlag.description: Is there a conflicting claim elsewhere in the register

contradicts.description: Array of claimId refs if flagged

executiveSummary.description: One-page overview for the top of the printed report

headline.description: The single most important thing a bid team should know

overview.description: 2-3 paragraph summary of who they are and where they operate

scoreRationales.description: One sentence per headline score explaining the RAG rating. All six scores represented — three AI-assessed, three computed.

keyRisks.description: 2-3 most important items from the risk exposure profile, surfaced to the front page

clientProfiles.description: Client profiles for this supplier's key buyers

sectorBriefs.description: Sector briefs covering sectors this supplier operates in

pipelineScans.description: Pipeline scans relevant to this supplier's markets

scoreCard.description: A headline score displayed as a 4-colour RAG rating with rationale. Factor-level detail retained for machine consumption by Agent 3 — never shown as numbers in the human report.

rating.description: red = material concern or significant gap. amber = notable but manageable. green = healthy, well evidenced. grey = insufficient evidence to assess.

rationale.description: One sentence explaining the rating — what drove it to this colour

factors.description: Machine-readable factor breakdown for Agent 3 consumption. Not rendered in the human report. For computed scores, factors are populated by the renderer. For AI-assessed scores, factors are populated by the model.

evidenced.description: True if this factor has supporting evidence

evidenceSummary.description: Key evidence, or 'No evidence available' if unevidenced
